metadata.dispatch_line: Possible stalking in progress, 2456 Sakura Avenue, report of a male subject following a female, Everett, Washington

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible stalking in progress, 2456 Sakura Avenue, report of a male subject following a female, Everett, Washington

facts.stalking_01: Time of incident: 02:45 AM, Time of dispatch: 02:50 AM, Time of arrival: 03:05 AM
facts.stalking_02: The parties are in an ex-intimate partner relationship, having dated for 3 years prior to the incident
facts.stalking_03: History of relationship: frequent verbal arguments, 2 prior incidents of physical violence, and ongoing intimidation via text messages and social media
facts.stalking_04: Stalking behaviors: following the victim to work, repeated unwanted phone calls, and sending unsolicited gifts to the victim's residence
facts.stalking_05: Valid protection order in place, verified through the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) database, Order number: PO-2023-001
facts.stalking_06: All witnesses interviewed: Jane Doe (neighbor), John Smith (victim's coworker), and documented in the report
facts.stalking_07: Previous incidents documented: 2 prior reports of stalking, 1 prior report of physical violence
facts.stalking_08: Evidence collected: torn clothing, broken phone, and a threatening letter from the suspect
facts.stalking_09: Electronic/technological evidence collected: phone records, text messages, and social media posts from the suspect's accounts
facts.stalking_10: All incidents of stalking behavior captured: 15 phone calls, 20 text messages, and 5 social media posts within the past 48 hours
facts.stalking_11: Scene described: 2456 Sakura Avenue, Everett, Washington, a single-family residence with a front and back yard
facts.stalking_12: Weapons/objects used: none visible, but the suspect has access to a firearm, as documented in the report
facts.stalking_13: Emotional state of the victim: fearful, anxious, and feeling trapped
facts.stalking_14: Evidence of fear articulated: the victim reported being 'scared for her life' and 'feeling like she's being watched all the time'
facts.stalking_15: All threats clearly documented: the suspect threatened to 'hurt her if she didn't take him back' and 'make her life miserable'
facts.stalking_16: Use of coercion and/or force articulated: the suspect used guilt and emotional manipulation to control the victim
facts.stalking_17: All injuries properly documented: the victim reported a bruise on her left arm and a scratch on her right cheek
facts.stalking_18: Property damage documented: the suspect broke the victim's phone and damaged her front door
facts.stalking_19: Photos taken and details recorded: photos of the victim's injuries, the damaged property, and the scene
facts.stalking_20: Victim's report of being strangled: the victim reported being choked by the suspect during a prior incident
facts.stalking_21: Victim's need for medical attention documented: the victim was treated for her injuries at the scene and transported to the hospital for further evaluation
facts.stalking_22: All spontaneous statements captured: the victim stated 'I'm scared, I just want him to leave me alone'
facts.stalking_23: Elements of the stalking crime thoroughly articulated: the suspect's behavior meets the criteria for stalking, as defined by the relevant state statute
facts.stalking_24: Victim's safety planning documented: the victim was provided with a safety plan, including a temporary restraining order and emergency contact information
facts.stalking_25: Information about obtaining an order of protection provided: the victim was informed about the process of obtaining a permanent restraining order
facts.stalking_26: Information about local service providers supplied: the victim was given contact information for the National Domestic Violence Hotline and the local women's shelter
facts.stalking_27: Threat/risk assessment tool utilized: the officer used the Danger Assessment tool to assess the victim's risk of further harm
facts.stalking_28: Use of a stalking kit and/or stalking log discussed: the officer discussed the use of a stalking log with the victim to document future incidents
facts.stalking_29: Suspect's access to weapons documented: the suspect has a firearm registered in his name, as verified through the relevant state database
facts.stalking_30: Emotional state of the suspect: the suspect was calm and cooperative during the interview, but became agitated when confronted with the evidence
facts.stalking_31: Suspect's history of protection orders: the suspect has a prior history of protection orders, with 2 previous orders issued against him
facts.stalking_32: Suspect's prohibition from possessing firearms: the suspect is prohibited from possessing firearms due to the valid protection order in place, as per 18 U.S.C. § 922(g)(8), and potentially due to a conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence, if applicable
facts.stalking_33: Active warrants out for the suspect documented: there is an active warrant for the suspect's arrest, issued on 02/10/2023, for a prior incident of domestic violence

interview_transcript.content: Hello Ms. Morales, I am Officer Baumgartner.  Can you tell me what happened?

(nervously) Oh, officer... I'm so scared. It's been going on for a while now, but last night was the worst. My ex, he's been stalking me, following me to work, calling me nonstop, sending me gifts... it's just so creepy. Last night, he showed up at my house, and I was so frightened. I tried to stay inside, but he just kept calling and texting me, threatening me. I was so scared, I didn't know what to do. (starting to tear up) I just wanted him to leave me alone. I have a protection order against him, but it doesn't seem to be stopping him. (voice shaking) I'm just really scared, officer. I don't know what he's capable of.

(sniffling) His name is... (pausing, collecting herself) I don't know if I should say. I'm scared of what he might do if he finds out I'm talking to the police. (looking around nervously) But I guess I have to, right? (taking a deep breath) His name is... (hesitating, then whispering) Alex. We were together for three years, and it was... (shaking her head) it was a really bad relationship.
What is Alex's last name?  Do you know his birthdate?

(hesitating, then answering) His last name is... (pausing, then saying) I don't know if I should give you that information. (looking at the officer nervously) But I suppose it's already on the protection order, isn't it? (sighing) It's... (whispering) Ramirez. Alex Ramirez. As for his birthdate... (thinking for a moment) I think it's... (hesitating, then saying) March 12, 1992. But I'm not really sure, officer. We didn't really celebrate his birthday or anything like that. (shaking her head) He was never really into that sort of thing.
Can you tell me more about the threats that Alex has made?

(looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) He's said some really scary things to me, officer. He's threatened to hurt me if I don't take him back, if I don't stop seeing other people... (shuddering) He's said he'll make my life miserable, that I'll never be able to get away from him. He's even said that he'll hurt my family if I don't do what he wants. (looking up, tears in her eyes) He's been sending me messages, calling me, showing up at my work... it's like he's always watching me, waiting for me to slip up so he can pounce. (her voice cracking) I'm so scared, officer. I feel like I'm walking around with a target on my back all the time.

(taking a deep breath, trying to composed herself) He's also sent me some really disturbing messages, officer. He's told me that he knows where I live, where I work, where my family lives... (shaking her head) It's like he's trying to intimidate me, to make me feel like I'm trapped and can't escape. (her voice dropping to a whisper) And the worst part is, he's been saying that he'll do whatever it takes to get me back, no matter what it takes. That's what really scares me, officer. I don't know what he's capable of.
Has Alex ever physically harmed you?

(hesitating, looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) Yes... he has. (pausing, collecting herself) There have been a few times when he's gotten physical with me. (her voice cracking) He's pushed me, grabbed me, and... (swallowing hard) and choked me once. It was really scary, officer. I thought he was going to... (shaking her head, unable to finish the sentence)

(taking a deep breath, trying to compose herself) It happened a few months ago, when we were still together. We were arguing, and he just lost control. He grabbed me by the throat and... (touching her neck, wincing at the memory) it was really painful. I thought I was going to pass out. (looking up, tears in her eyes) I was so scared, officer. I didn't know what to do.

(her voice dropping to a whisper) And even after we broke up, he's still been physical with me. He's shown up at my house, tried to force his way in... (shuddering) it's been really terrifying, officer. I just want him to leave me alone.
Do you know where Alex lives?

(thinking for a moment) I'm not really sure, officer. We didn't live together, and after we broke up, I didn't really keep track of where he was living. (pausing) But I think he might still be living in the same apartment we used to visit when we were together. It was in a complex off of... (trying to remember) I think it was Evergreen Drive? Or maybe it was Oak Street? (looking uncertain) I'm sorry, officer, I'm not really sure. It's been a while since I've been there.

(looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) To be honest, I've been trying to avoid thinking about him and where he might be. It's just easier for me to try to move on and forget about the whole thing. (looking up, tears in her eyes) But I know that's not an option now, is it?
Do you know when the protection order was issued?

(nodding) Yes, officer. The protection order was issued... (thinking for a moment) I think it was earlier this year. (pausing, trying to remember) Yes, it was after one of the incidents where he showed up at my house and tried to force his way in. I went to the courthouse and filed for a protection order, and it was granted. (looking relieved) It's been a big help, officer. At least I know that if he shows up again, I can call the police and they'll do something about it.

(looking down, her voice barely above a whisper) But even with the protection order, I'm still scared, officer. I'm scared that he'll find a way to get around it, that he'll hurt me anyway. (looking up, tears in her eyes) That's why I'm so glad you're here, officer. I just want to feel safe again.
Do you have a safe place to stay until we can contact Alex?

(nodding) Yes, officer. I have a friend who lives on the other side of town, and I can stay with her for a while. (thinking for a moment) Her name is Sarah, and she's been really supportive throughout all of this. I've been staying with her on and off for a few weeks now, whenever I feel like Alex is getting too close. (looking relieved) She's got a really secure apartment, and I feel safe there.

(pulling out a piece of paper from her pocket) I've also got the number for the local women's shelter, just in case. (showing the paper to the officer) I was given this number by a counselor at the courthouse when I got the protection order. She told me that if I ever needed a safe place to stay, I could call them and they would help me out. (looking up, hopeful) Do you think I'll need to go there, officer? Or can I just stay with Sarah for now?
Has Alex texted you recently, and if so, do you have the text messages saved?

(nodding) Yes, officer. Alex has been texting me a lot lately. (pulling out her phone) I've been saving all of the messages, just in case I need to show them to someone. (scrolling through her phone) Let me see... (finding the messages) Yes, here they are. (showing the officer her phone) He's been sending me all sorts of messages, from sweet and romantic ones to really threatening and scary ones. (pointing to a message on her phone) This one, for example, says "I'll never let you go" and "You'll always be mine". (looking up at the officer) It's really creepy, officer.

(continuing to scroll through her phone) I've also got messages where he's telling me that he knows where I am, and that he's watching me. (showing the officer another message) This one says "I saw you at the grocery store yesterday" and "You look beautiful". (shuddering) It's just really scary, officer, because I know he's been following me.

(looking up at the officer) I've been trying to document everything, just in case I need to show it to someone. (nervously) Do you think this will help, officer? Will this be enough to... (trailing off, hoping the officer will be able to help her)

narrative:
Police Report
Incident Information
Crime Type: Stalking

Dispatch Call Type: Possible stalking in progress

Location: 2456 Sakura Avenue, Everett, Washington

Reporting Officer: Officer Baumgartner

Interviewee / Victim: Leila Morales-Ramirez

Narrative
On the above call, Officer Baumgartner contacted Leila Morales-Ramirez regarding a stalking complaint at 2456 Sakura Avenue in Everett, Washington. Morales-Ramirez stated she was fearful and reported that her ex-boyfriend, identified as Alex Ramirez, had been stalking her for some time. She said his conduct included following her to work, repeatedly calling her, sending unwanted gifts, and appearing at her residence.

Morales-Ramirez reported that the prior night was the worst incident. She stated Alex Ramirez came to her house, and while she remained inside, he repeatedly called and texted her and made threatening statements. She said she was in fear and wanted him to leave her alone.

Morales-Ramirez stated she currently has a protection order against Alex Ramirez, but his behavior has continued despite that order. She said she fears what he may do and believes he is capable of harming her.

Morales-Ramirez identified the suspect as Alex Ramirez, possibly born March 12, 1992, though she said she was not certain of the birthdate. She stated they had been in a relationship for approximately three years and described it as a bad relationship.

When asked about threats, Morales-Ramirez stated Alex Ramirez told her he would hurt her if she did not take him back and if she continued seeing other people. She further stated he threatened to make her life miserable, said she would never get away from him, and threatened to hurt her family if she did not comply with his demands. She also reported receiving disturbing messages in which he said he knew where she lived, where she worked, and where her family lived, and that he would do whatever it takes to get her back.

Morales-Ramirez disclosed prior physical abuse by Alex Ramirez during the relationship. She reported that on prior occasions he pushed her, grabbed her, and choked her once during an argument several months ago. She stated he grabbed her by the throat, causing pain and making her feel as though she might pass out. She also reported that after the breakup, he showed up at her residence and tried to force his way inside.

Morales-Ramirez stated she was unsure of Alex Ramirez's current address. She believed he might still live at an apartment complex they used to visit, possibly off Evergreen Drive or Oak Street, but she was uncertain.

Regarding the protection order, Morales-Ramirez said it was issued earlier this year after an incident in which Alex Ramirez showed up at her house and tried to force his way in. She stated she obtained the order through the courthouse.

Morales-Ramirez said she has a safe place to stay with a friend named Sarah on the other side of town and has been staying there on and off for several weeks when she felt unsafe. She also stated she had contact information for a local women's shelter provided by a courthouse counselor.

Morales-Ramirez showed her phone to Officer Baumgartner and stated she had saved recent text messages from Alex Ramirez. She reported the messages ranged from romantic to threatening. She specifically pointed out messages stating, "I'll never let you go," "You'll always be mine," and messages indicating he had seen her at a grocery store and was watching her. Morales-Ramirez stated she had been documenting the messages for evidence.

Suspect Information
Name: Alex Ramirez

DOB: Possible March 12, 1992 (victim unsure)

Relationship to Victim: Ex-boyfriend

Possible Residence: Apartment complex possibly near Evergreen Drive or Oak Street; exact location unknown

Evidence
Victim statement

Saved text messages on victim's phone

Existing protection order

Safety / Follow-Up
Victim expressed fear for her safety and for the safety of her family.

Victim stated she can stay with a friend named Sarah in a secure apartment.

Victim also has contact information for a local women's shelter.

Recommended follow-up includes collection of digital message evidence, verification of protection order status, and attempts to identify and locate the suspect.
